meaning: adj.喧扰的,喜欢吵闹的,摇晃的,不牢固的
examples: Raised in a rackety household with a sick mother and alcoholic father, the young Ms Harris sought comfort in animals; snakes, bugs, stray dogs, cats.
Born into a wealthy family in 1928, he studied to be a doctor, but chose instead the infinitely more rackety and less respectable life of a manga cartoonist.
Haymarket's frantic, rackety Chop Chop (chop-chop.co.uk) serves some of the best dumplings I've tasted outside Hong Kong.
Poland wants Ukraine as a prosperous, stable and accessible neighbour, not as a poor and rackety one with a dodgy democracy and even dodgier nuclear power stations.
